title: Pregnancy-safe body oil
subtitle: A gentle unscented or lightly lavender-scented body oil for use during pregnancy
excerpt: This blend uses only carrier oils known to be safe throughout pregnancy, with lavender essential oil at a conservative 0.5% dilution: a rate considered safe from the second trimester onwards. Sweet almond oil and fractionated coconut oil are light, quickly absorbed, and gentle on sensitive skin.
sourceNotes: EO safety in pregnancy from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), chapter on reproductive safety. Sweet almond oil properties from cosmetic supplier datasheet. Lavender EO pregnancy caution from Tisserand Institute (tisserandinstitute.org, May 2026).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. For an unscented version, omit the lavender essential oil entirely.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in advance and store in a pump bottle. Apply to damp skin after a shower for best absorption.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a cool, dark place. Sweet almond oil has a shorter shelf life than jojoba: the limiting factor here. Vitamin E extends stability. Discard if the oil develops a rancid smell.

recipeTools.notes: Or any small jug for blending

glossaryTerms.definition: An ingredient that softens and smooths the skin by filling gaps in the stratum corneum and reducing water loss. Carrier oils act as emollients. They do not add moisture to the skin but retain what the skin already has by forming a barrier layer on the surface.

content.text: During pregnancy, the list of essential oils to avoid is long and the research on specific risk levels is limited. The safest approach is to keep any essential oil use minimal and restricted to oils with good safety records. Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia) has one of the longest records of safe use in pregnancy at low dilutions: 0.5% is the conservative standard. Avoid the first trimester entirely for any scented product. This formula is also fully effective as an unscented
oil: simply omit the lavender for a scent-free version.
Sweet almond oil contains a tree-nut allergen. For those with tree-nut allergies, substitute an equal weight of fractionated coconut oil or jojoba oil, both of which are nut-free.

items.prepNote: Light, skin-conditioning carrier; contains tree-nut allergen
Antioxidant; extends shelf life and conditions the skin
0.5% dilution; Lavandula angustifolia only; from second trimester onwards; omit in first trimester

content.text: Combine all ingredients in a small jug and stir gently. Pour into a pump bottle or a small bottle with a flip top. Label with the date and the EO details if scented. Apply to damp skin after showering, working in sections and allowing the oil to absorb before dressing. For use on the abdomen, apply in gentle circular movements.

12 months at room temperature away from direct sunlight. The sweet almond oil is the stability-limiting ingredient. Discard if the oil smells sharp or rancid. No water content means no preservative is required.
